│                      ▝▜█████▛▘                     │ Added `--bare` flag for scripted `-p` calls — skips hooks, LSP, plugin sync, and skill directory walks; requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` or an `apiKeyHelper` via `--settings` (OAuth and keychain auth disabled); auto-memory fully disabled │
│                        ▘▘ ▝▝                       │ Added `--channels` permission relay — channel servers that declare the permission capability can forward tool approval prompts to your phone                                                                                              │

❯ /plan
  ⎿  Enabled plan mode

❯ currently in `exec` pane in `viz`, we have pathetically little amount of data rendered. It looks to me that we're struggling to reconstruct the execution observed [Image #1] - eg here we only see little tiny splits of the order placement at the open and close of the position I imagine, but I'm not seeing any order
  movement in-beetwen? Is this the fault of our execution logic or the rendering? Oh, actually, I guess the only way to find out is to make a new integration test in ./examples/, that would use `v_utils` to generate a random walk using `laplace` distribution (check in ~/s/v_utils, we already have at least one in
  tests), - and then have a test strategy, that just buys and runs a trailing stop 1% below the price or what not. And then additionally do Poisson sampling of the values from the underlying laplacian we gen, so we get more natural feel of orders coming in irregularly. And then we should be able to run a backtest over
  it, and have our `Mock` strategy get into the execution mode and follow it. And then just ensure that we are able to display our orders generated over this distribution properly. Actually, changed my mind on underlying, - let's randomly short/long it when the price has changed over 5% since last entry, and then we
  trail with 0.1% price. During the generated period, I want on average 10 entries, each of which should last for ~2-5m translating to ~1000 ticks // this should replace the current FIXTURE thing, - what we have now there is a bunch of useless drawings

  // while at it, make it so that current `right/left` errors are replaced with `up/down`, - now they'll be going through the situations. And `right/left` will only work in `exec` mode, - it'll go through trade instances within a situation

  Thought for 10s (ctrl+o to expand)

● I'll explore the codebase to understand the request. Let me launch parallel Explore agents.

● 3 Explore agents finished (ctrl+o to expand)
   ├ Explore viz frontend exec pane · 16 tool uses · 110.2k tokens
   │ ⎿  Done
   ├ Explore backtest execution Mock strategy · 30 tool uses · 124.9k tokens
   │ ⎿  Done
   └ Explore v_utils laplace random walk · 27 tool uses · 86.6k tokens
     ⎿  Done

  Thought for 4m 34s (ctrl+o to expand)

● Good foundation. Let me read the critical files directly to nail down the design—especially the fixture generator, the keyboard nav, the persist/order schema, and the execution trailing logic.

  Thought for 5s, read 2 files (ctrl+o to expand)

● Let me understand episodes/exec navigation and the persist schemas for orders/trading/sizing.

  Thought for 52s, searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

● Let me check how episodes are discovered (backend) and how exec renders orders, plus the trades/bars_1s source—this determines exactly which streams the fixture must emit.

  Thought for 1m 32s, searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

● I have a complete picture of every on-disk stream format, the exec pane's data sources, and the keyboard nav. One genuine fork remains: the request says "run a backtest ... Mock strategy get into execution mode," but there is no Mock strategy in the codebase, and the real execution engine (LiquidationsExec) is
  classification-driven and trails the book top — it cannot do "random long/short on a 5% move, trail 0.1%." That described behavior is a synthetic mock. Let me confirm the approach before finalizing.

● User answered Claude's questions:
  ⎿  · The described strategy (random long/short on >5% move, trail 0.1%, ~10 entries) doesn't match the real execution engine (LiquidationsExec is classification-driven, single-category, trails the book). How should the example produce the order/trade/PnL streams the exec pane reads? → Drive the real BacktestEngine
  ⎿  Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?

And let's run our own strat there, why not, - we'll just focus on rendering it correctly instead of
